query_normalized: exploring the validity of the weak equivalence principle with publicly available planetary orbital data physics
rationale: This paper addresses the core mechanism of the user's question by investigating the Weak Equivalence Principle (WEP) and its potential violations, satisfying criterion (a) for same-mechanism evidence. Although it focuses on theoretical finite-temperature effects rather than empirical planetary data, it is a foundational theoretical work on the specific physical construct (WEP validity) that the user is exploring.
